statement.p: There are three pipes at a tank. To fill the tank, it would take Pipe A 10 hours, Pipe B 15 hours, and Pipe C 12 hours. Answer the following question:
If all three pipes are turned on, it would take hours to fill the tank.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a pipe 5 hours to fill a tank. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, the pipe would fill \frac{1}{5} of the tank.
Similarly, if it takes a pipe x hours to fill a tank, each hour, the pipe would fill \frac{1}{x} of the tank.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Its given that it takes Pipe A 10 hours to fill the tank. Each hour, Pipe A fills \frac{1}{10} of the tank.
Similarly, each hour, Pipe B fills \frac{1}{15} of the tank; and Pipe C fills \frac{1}{12} of the tank.
Assume that, if all pipes are turned on, it would take x hours to fill the tank. Thus, each hour, they can fill \frac{1}{x} of the tank.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:
In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {60x}.
By the solution we got, if all pipe are turned on, it would take {4} hours to fill the tank.
